needle_load.note: Claude includes a 5s idle window before instrument-in then 13s of needle handling; GPT-4o starts the clock at first instrument visible and assigns only 4s. Sum of needle_load + suture_placement is similar (Claude 55s vs GPT-4o 40s).

second_throw.note: Major divergence — Claude allocates only 22s, GPT-4o 44s. Combined with third_throw, the totals are closer (Claude 60s vs GPT-4o 70s).

third_throw.note: Inverted from the systematic_finding pattern — here GPT-4o is LOWER on third_throw, not higher. The slow segment exists, but the two teachers disagree about whether it lives in throw 2 or throw 3.

suture_cut.note: Both teachers identify cutting as the dominant inefficiency.

placement_alignment.rationale: Both teachers report low-confidence placement near 1-1.5mm per mark. Reconciled at 2.75mm.

reconciliation.method: average completion time, average penalties, formula = 600 - time - penalties
reconciliation.rationale: Both teachers agree on the qualitative picture: clean closure, all knots secure, hand switches preserved, no avulsion or gap; bottleneck is suture cutting (44-49s) plus a slow knot segment (~22s disputed between throw 2 and throw 3). The 4.8 FLS delta is well within the V7/post2 calibration band (3.5-4.9) and qualifies as strong agreement.

calibration_notes.interpretation: Four of five comparisons now fall in the 0.3-4.9 ΔFLS band. V8 remains the lone outlier and the outlier is well-explained by Claude's 62s suture_placement default failure mode under uncertainty.

gpt4o_third_throw_inflation.previous_observation: GPT-4o systematically inflates third_throw duration relative to Claude when there is reinforcing manipulation to attribute (post2 46.2 vs 30; V7 51 vs 24; V8 67 vs 30); does NOT apply when bottleneck is unambiguously elsewhere (post_practice).
gpt4o_third_throw_inflation.new_data_point: post4 / V4: GPT-4o assigns 26s third_throw, Claude assigns 38s. The inflation pattern is INVERTED here — GPT-4o instead inflates the second throw (44s vs 22s).
gpt4o_third_throw_inflation.refined_hypothesis: GPT-4o inflates ONE knot segment when there is extra manipulation to attribute, but the segment it picks (throw 2 vs throw 3) is not stable. Combined throw-2-plus-throw-3 duration is more reliable than either segment in isolation. The critique agent should prefer the SUM of throws 2+3 over individual phase boundaries when the two teachers disagree internally.
